name: Dijon-Style Mustard
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, stir together mustard powder and water to form a paste.
2. In a saucepan, combine vinegar, wine, onion, shallots, garlic, bay leaf, peppercorns, and juniper berries. Bring the mixture to a simmer over moderate heat.
3. Simmer until reduced by two-thirds. Strain the mixture, cover, and chill.
4. Stir the chilled reduction into the mustard paste. Add lemon juice, salt, and sugar; stir to combine.
5. Let the mixture stand for at least 20 minutes. Transfer to a saucepan and bring to a simmer over low heat. Cook for 15 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and allow to cool. Transfer to a sterile jar and seal tightly. Store on a dark, cool shelf for at least a month or up to six weeks before using. The mustard will mellow with age.
7. Once opened, refrigerate and use within six months.
